extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC Primož won from the early break after soloing from Telegraph/Galibier through terrifying descents, holding off a chasing Froome/Uran group.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC crashed in breakaway on soft sand but still had power reserves to rampage climbs, suggesting exceptional recovery capacity.
MARTIN Dan's repeated attacks softened field before BARGUIL/BARDET offensive, revealing deliberate AG2R staged assault pattern at altitude.
SKY maintained two riders (FROOME, LANDA) in finale despite multiple accelerations, unprecedented luxury for GC protection on HC climbs.
BARGUIL Warren prioritized KOM points over stage win by attacking for third behind breakaway, sacrificing GC position for jersey mathematics.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primož: positive: Five years cycling experience yet won hardest stage, descended Galibier fearlessly, time-trialed to hold off GC group
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Only GC rider excited for Alps pre-stage, responded personally when LANDA couldn't, gained 9 seconds on rivals
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: Dropped definitively on Galibier accelerations, lost GC podium position to URAN who handled altitude better
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: Never saw front again after early accelerations at altitude, white jersey lead now vulnerable despite TT advantage
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: positive: Crashed, got shredded, still attacked for third on Galibier securing 49-point KOM lead with double-points finish remaining
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Hot hand final week made KDH crash abandonment decisive for green jersey, Sagan-like versatility emerging
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: negative: Five stage wins but abandoned after crash, nine-point deficit would have made green jersey contestable
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Offensive forced KWIATKOWSKI to exhaustion before BARDET/BARGUIL attacks, tactical sacrificial role for AG2R
rider_signals.MENTIES Louis: positive: Clawed back to YATES after being distanced, closed white jersey gap before final mountain stage
